title: ops/slides.py: write operations and pptx_edit context manager
description: Extend `pypptx/ops/slides.py` with the `pptx_edit(path)` context manager and write operations: `add_slide()`, `delete_slide()`, and `move_slide()`. The context manager unpacks to a temp dir on enter; on normal exit it cleans and repacks atomically; on exception it leaves the original file untouched. `add_slide` handles both `--duplicate` (strip notes relationship from copy) and `--layout` modes. All indexing is 1-based at the interface boundary.
acceptance_criteria: `pptx_edit()` is a context manager yielding the temp unpacked path.
On clean exit: `clean_unused_files()` is called, then the directory is repacked over the original atomically.
On exception: the original `.pptx` is not modified.
`add_slide(path, duplicate=N)` strips the notes-slide relationship from the duplicated slide's `.rels`.
`add_slide(path, layout=N)` creates a blank slide from the specified layout index.
`delete_slide(path, index)` removes the slide at 1-based index and returns `{deleted_file, deleted_index}`.
`move_slide(path, from_idx, to_idx)` reorders `sldIdLst` and returns `{file, from, to}`.
Exactly one of `duplicate` or `layout` must be provided to `add_slide`; raises `ValueError` otherwise.
